title: Circle Furniture to auction off remaining inventory after bankruptcy
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Circle Furniture to auction its remaining inventory after filing for bankruptcy, involving hundreds of showroom and warehouse items.
Online auctions hosted by Paul E. Spaerstein Co. run from March 19 to April 29, covering store locations and warehouses.
The retailer reported assets of $2.27 million against nearly $14 million in liabilities, affecting creditors and customers.

title: Watch the N.H. Liquor & Wine Outlet video that’s lowkey going viral
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New Hampshire Liquor & Wine Outlet's promotional video reached over a million views on Facebook and Instagram.
The video uses Gen Z phrases and stars marketing director Mark Roy to connect with younger audiences.
The outlet hosts sold-out events like 'Sommelier Select' and 'Perfect Pairings' to attract younger consumers.

title: Bruins trade deadline live updates: Will Don Sweeney add or subtract from Boston’s roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins face a trade deadline decision with a tight playoff race, one point ahead of Columbus Blue Jackets.
Bruins made a minor trade, sending Brett Harrison and Jackson Edward to Flyers for Alexis Gendron and Massimo Rizzo.
No confirmation on acquiring defensemen like Johnathan Kovacevic or Rasmus Ristolainen, amid ongoing trade discussions.

title: Job interviews now include AI and personality tests. Here’s what candidates should know.
author: Elaine Varelas
bullets: AI and personality tests are increasingly used in job interviews, posing potential legal risks, especially around deception assessment tools.
82% of companies use assessment tools in hiring, focusing on skills, cultural fit, and job retention, says the Society for Human Resource Management.
Massachusetts law challenges AI interview tools analyzing traits like 'integrity', with cases citing them as unlawful lie-detector tests.

title: She’s running her 11th Boston Marathon for her son and others affected by addiction
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jill Borthwick, 72, will run her 11th Boston Marathon in memory of her late son, John O’Brien.
Borthwick dedicates this year's run to the Herren Project, supporting addiction awareness and combating stigma.
Inspired by the film 'Unguarded,' she's emotionally prepared to honor those affected by addiction.

title: ‘If he scores 34 goals, I’ll eat my hat’: Radio host’s boast in spotlight after Morgan Geekie’s milestone goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morgan Geekie scored his 34th goal of the season in the Bruins' 6-3 loss to the Predators.
Michael Felger doubted Geekie's goal-scoring potential, claiming he would eat his hat if Geekie reached 34 goals.
Felger's comments surfaced online after Sports Hub producer Kevin Maggiore highlighted the milestone achievement.

title: Man accused of assaulting students located by Boston police
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Seitu Morel, 32, allegedly assaulted students from Eliot Upper School near TD Garden in Boston on Wednesday.
After the alleged assaults, Morel attempted to steal a bag from a charter bus near TD Garden.
Boston police obtained arrest warrants for Morel and successfully located him after seeking public assistance.

title: Early bookings are strong for Cape Cod summer rentals
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Early bookings for Cape Cod rentals are strong, with WeNeedAVacation.com reporting a 20% increase over last year.
WeNeedAVacation.com notes 70% of prime summer weeks were over 50% booked by January 2026.
Rental prices on Cape Cod are rising 3-6% overall, with waterfront properties seeing higher price increases.

title: Ordering DoorDash in Boston? Here’s how much more you’re really paying.
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston diners pay up to 80% more using delivery apps like DoorDash, compared to picking up takeout.
Delivery apps charge Boston restaurants a commission of 15-30%, leading to marked-up menu prices on the apps.
Restaurants recommend self-pickup or dining in to avoid delivery fees and maintain food quality.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com launched a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston events.
Peter Chianca has served as Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
The quiz covers various trending stories affecting the Greater Boston community.
